source_paper_title: Online Estimation and Optimization of Utility-Based Shortfall Risk
source_paper_bibtex: @article{Hegde_2025, title={Online Estimation and Optimization of Utility-Based Shortfall Risk}, volume={50}, ISSN={1526-5471}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1287/moor.2022.0266}, DOI={10.1287/moor.2022.0266}, number={4}, journal={Mathematics of Operations Research}, publisher={Institute for Operations Research and the Management Sciences (INFORMS)}, author={Hegde, Vishwajit and Menon, Arvind Satish and Prashanth, L.A. and Jagannathan, Krishna}, year={2025}, month=nov, pages={2470–2501} }

Third, it remains open to extend our finite sample bounds to the estimator of multivariate shortfall risk measure (MSRM) [2]. In a recent work [41], the authors derive asymptotic consistency and normality results for an estimator of MSRM, while finite sample bounds are not available.
title: Finite-sample bounds for multivariate shortfall risk estimators with streaming samples
background_latex:
Let \((\Omega,\mathcal F,\mathbb P)\) be a probability space. Fix an integer dimension \(d\ge 2\). Let \(X\in L^\infty(\Omega;\mathbb R^d)\) be a bounded \(d\)-dimensional random vector representing (componentwise) losses or positions.

Let \(\ell:\mathbb R^d\to\mathbb R\) be a convex loss/utility-penalty function (sometimes called an aggregation loss), and let \(\lambda\in\mathbb R\) be a fixed risk-level parameter (assumed to be in the interior of the range of \(\ell\) under the law of the argument in question).

Define the acceptance set
\[
\mathcal A := \bigl\{Y\in L^\infty(\Omega;\mathbb R^d): \ \mathbb E[\,\ell(-Y)\,] \le \lambda\bigr\}.
\]

A multivariate shortfall risk measure (MSRM) is a multivariate analogue of utility-based shortfall risk, defined via the cash-additivity/translation of the acceptance set: for a given position \(X\), one seeks a deterministic capital allocation vector \(m\in\mathbb R^d\) such that \(X+m\in\mathcal A\); one may consider the set of admissible allocations
\[
\mathcal R_\lambda(X) := \{m\in\mathbb R^d : \mathbb E[\ell(-(X+m))]\le \lambda\},
\]
and/or a scalarized risk value derived from \(\mathcal R_\lambda(X)\) (the precise scalarization depends on the MSRM definition).

Assume an online/streaming observation model: samples \(\{X_i\}_{i\ge 1}\) are revealed one-at-a-time, where \(X_i\) are i.i.d. copies of \(X\). An estimator for an MSRM is any measurable mapping \(\widehat{\mathcal R}_{\lambda,n}\) (or \(\widehat m_n\)) computed from \(X_1,\dots,X_n\). “Finite-sample bounds” refers to non-asymptotic guarantees (e.g., mean-squared error or concentration inequalities) as explicit functions of \(n\).
problem_definition_latex:
Develop non-asymptotic (finite-sample) performance guarantees for an online/streaming estimator of a multivariate shortfall risk measure (MSRM) based on i.i.d. samples \(X_1,\dots,X_n\) of a bounded random vector \(X\in\mathbb R^d\).

Concretely, provide explicit bounds (in \(n\)) on the estimation error of an MSRM estimator \(\widehat{\mathcal R}_{\lambda,n}\) (or an estimated capital allocation \(\widehat m_n\in\mathbb R^d\)) relative to the target MSRM determined by \(\ell\) and \(\lambda\), analogous in spirit to finite-sample bounds available for the univariate utility-based shortfall risk estimator.
latex:
Let \((\Omega,\mathcal F,\mathbb P)\) be a probability space. Fix an integer dimension \(d\ge 2\). Let \(X\in L^\infty(\Omega;\mathbb R^d)\) be a bounded \(d\)-dimensional random vector representing (componentwise) losses or positions.

Let \(\ell:\mathbb R^d\to\mathbb R\) be a convex loss/utility-penalty function (sometimes called an aggregation loss), and let \(\lambda\in\mathbb R\) be a fixed risk-level parameter (assumed to be in the interior of the range of \(\ell\) under the law of the argument in question).

Define the acceptance set
\[
\mathcal A := \bigl\{Y\in L^\infty(\Omega;\mathbb R^d): \ \mathbb E[\,\ell(-Y)\,] \le \lambda\bigr\}.
\]

A multivariate shortfall risk measure (MSRM) is a multivariate analogue of utility-based shortfall risk, defined via the cash-additivity/translation of the acceptance set: for a given position \(X\), one seeks a deterministic capital allocation vector \(m\in\mathbb R^d\) such that \(X+m\in\mathcal A\); one may consider the set of admissible allocations
\[

and/or a scalarized risk value derived from \(\mathcal R_\lambda(X)\) (the precise scalarization depends on the MSRM definition).

Assume an online/streaming observation model: samples \(\{X_i\}_{i\ge 1}\) are revealed one-at-a-time, where \(X_i\) are i.i.d. copies of \(X\). An estimator for an MSRM is any measurable mapping \(\widehat{\mathcal R}_{\lambda,n}\) (or \(\widehat m_n\)) computed from \(X_1,\dots,X_n\). “Finite-sample bounds” refers to non-asymptotic guarantees (e.g., mean-squared error or concentration inequalities) as explicit functions of \(n\).

Develop non-asymptotic (finite-sample) performance guarantees for an online/streaming estimator of a multivariate shortfall risk measure (MSRM) based on i.i.d. samples \(X_1,\dots,X_n\) of a bounded random vector \(X\in\mathbb R^d\).

Concretely, provide explicit bounds (in \(n\)) on the estimation error of an MSRM estimator \(\widehat{\mathcal R}_{\lambda,n}\) (or an estimated capital allocation \(\widehat m_n\in\mathbb R^d\)) relative to the target MSRM determined by \(\ell\) and \(\lambda\), analogous in spirit to finite-sample bounds available for the univariate utility-based shortfall risk estimator.
location_in_paper: Page 33, Section 9 (Concluding Remarks and Future Work).
open_problem_quote: Third, it remains open to extend our finite sample bounds to the estimator of multivariate shortfall risk measure (MSRM) [2]. In a recent work [41], the authors derive asymptotic consistency and normality results for an estimator of MSRM, while finite sample bounds are not available.
counterexample_suitability_rationale: The problem asks for universal upper bounds (rates and constants) rather than existence of a counterexample. Automated search over instances is unlikely to falsify the statement, since the missing object is a proof technique and a bound that should hold uniformly over distributions satisfying assumptions.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: One can represent candidate MSRM estimators/recursions (e.g., projected stochastic approximation schemes in \(\mathbb R^d\)) and evaluate them empirically on simulated distributions with a computable proxy loss (empirical risk of constraint violation or distance to a high-accuracy offline solution). However, translating empirical performance into provable finite-sample bounds would still require substantial human proof work.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.

literature_review.synthesis:
The source paper (Hegde et al., 2023) develops a stochastic-approximation (SA) root-finding view of univariate utility-based shortfall risk (UBSR), where \(\mathrm{SR}_\lambda(X)\) is the unique root of \(g(t)=\mathbb E[\ell(-X-t)]-\lambda\). Under boundedness and a monotonicity condition on \(g\) (via bounds on \(-\ell'\)), it proves non-asymptotic mean-squared error rates \(\mathbb E[(t_n-\mathrm{SR}_\lambda(X))^2]=O(1/n)\) for stepsize \(a_n=c/n\) when the monotonicity parameter is known, and \(O(1/n^\alpha)\) for universal stepsizes \(a_n=c/n^\alpha\), together with high-probability concentration. It also analyzes biased ratio-form gradient estimators for UBSR optimization, again with explicit finite-sample rates.

Forward-citing work to date does not resolve the multivariate extension requested in the open problem: finite-sample bounds for streaming estimators of multivariate shortfall risk measures (MSRM) defined by \(\mathcal R_\lambda(X)=\{m\in\mathbb R^d: \mathbb E[\ell(-(X+m))]\le\lambda\}\) with \(d\ge2\) and convex aggregation loss \(\ell:\mathbb R^d\to\mathbb R\). The most relevant partial progress remains in the univariate/scalar setting: (i) learning/regression work provides concentration for SAA estimators of scalar shortfall risk under tail/margin conditions; (ii) risk-sensitive RL work provides \(O(1/m)\) MSE bounds for shortfall-risk policy-gradient estimators using double sampling; and (iii) optimization papers develop efficient solvers (e.g., ADMM and semismooth Newton projections) for finite-sample SAA programs. These contributions suggest tools—uniform concentration over \(m\in\mathbb R^d\), stability of feasible-set estimators, and SA/SGD analyses with biased or ratio estimators—but a complete non-asymptotic streaming theory for MSRM set/allocator estimation appears to remain open.

sources.title: Risk-sensitive reinforcement learning using expectiles, shortfall risk and optimized certainty equivalent risk
sources.theorem_or_result:
The paper derives non-asymptotic mean-squared error bounds of order \(O(1/m)\) for trajectory-based estimators of policy gradients for several scalar risk objectives, including utility-based shortfall risk (UBSR) \(\mathrm{SR}_{l,\lambda}(X)=\inf\{k\in\mathbb R: \mathbb E[l(X-k)]\le \lambda\}\). In particular, for a finite-horizon MDP with bounded cumulative cost \(F\), it proves a policy-gradient formula
\[\nabla \mathrm{SR}(\theta)=\frac{\mathbb E_\theta\big[l(F-\mathrm{SR}(\theta))\,G(\theta)\big]}{\mathbb E_\theta\big[l'(F-\mathrm{SR}(\theta))\big]}\]
(Theorem 6) and, using a double-sampling estimator \(Q^m_\theta(\hat Z,Z)\), establishes an MSE bound
\[\mathbb E\big[\|\nabla \mathrm{SR}(\theta)-Q^m_\theta(\hat Z,Z)\|_2^2\big]\le \frac{C}{m}\]
(Lemma 3), with explicit constants under boundedness/regularity assumptions. Analogous \(O(1/m)\) MSE bounds are shown for expectile and OCE gradient estimators (Theorem 5, Lemma 6).
sources.connection: The open problem asks for finite-sample (non-asymptotic) bounds for *multivariate* shortfall risk estimators \(\widehat{\mathcal R}_{\lambda,n}\) or capital allocations \(\hat m_n\in\mathbb R^d\) in a streaming i.i.d. setting for \(X\in\mathbb R^d\) and convex \(\ell:\mathbb R^d\to\mathbb R\). This paper provides finite-sample \(O(1/m)\) MSE bounds, but for *scalar* (univariate) UBSR/OCE/expectile objectives applied to the cumulative cost random variable \(F\) in RL, and for *gradient estimators* (policy optimization), not for estimating the multivariate allocation set \(\mathcal R_\lambda(X)\) or a multivariate MSRM from streaming samples. Thus it does not solve the multivariate streaming-estimation problem; it is partially relevant as it develops non-asymptotic analysis techniques for shortfall-type risk functionals and their sample-based root/ratio estimators, which could inform analogous bounds in the multivariate case.

sources.title: Optimizing Shortfall Risk Metric for Learning Regression Models
sources.theorem_or_result:
For univariate utility-based shortfall risk (UBSR) 
\(\mathrm{SR}_\lambda(Z)=\inf\{t\in\mathbb R: \mathbb E[\ell(Z-t)]\le \lambda\}\) with \(\ell\) convex, strictly increasing and \(G\)-Lipschitz, and assuming a bracketing condition (A1) giving \(t_L\le \mathrm{SR}_\lambda(Z)\le t_U\) with margin \(\eta>0\), the sample-average approximation estimator \(\mathrm{SR}_n\) defined by \(\min_t\{t: \frac1n\sum_{i=1}^n \ell(Z_i-t)\le \lambda\}\) satisfies concentration bounds:
- If \(Z\) is sub-Gaussian with parameter \(\sigma\), then with probability \(\ge 1-2\delta\),
\[|\mathrm{SR}_n-\mathrm{SR}_\lambda(Z)|\le \frac{2G\sigma (t_U-t_L)}{\eta}\sqrt{\frac{\log(1/\delta)}{n}}.\]
- If \(Z\) is sub-exponential with parameter \(K\), then with probability \(\ge 1-2\delta\),
\[|\mathrm{SR}_n-\mathrm{SR}_\lambda(Z)|\le \frac{4eGK (t_U-t_L)}{\eta}\frac{\log(1/\delta)}{n}.\]
Additionally, for regression with squared loss \(Z=(Y-\hat Y(X))^2\), their bisection Algorithm 1 returns \(h_T\) with excess UBSR bounded as
\[\mathrm{SR}_\lambda((Y-h_T(X))^2)\le \min_{h\in\mathcal H}\mathrm{SR}_\lambda((Y-h(X))^2)+C_1\rho+C_2\rho'+C_3 2^{-T},\]
where \(\rho\) is the optimization (ERM) suboptimality in each iteration and \(\rho'\) is the UBSR estimation error.
sources.connection:
The open problem asks for non-asymptotic finite-sample bounds for *multivariate* shortfall risk estimators (MSRM) with streaming samples \(X_i\in\mathbb R^d\), \(d\ge2\), and aggregation loss \(\ell:\mathbb R^d\to\mathbb R\). This paper provides finite-sample concentration bounds for the *univariate* UBSR estimator \(\mathrm{SR}_n\) (scalar \(Z\)) under sub-Gaussian/sub-exponential tails, and uses these bounds inside an algorithmic excess-risk guarantee for UBSR optimization in regression.

Thus it contributes techniques and explicit \(n\)-rates for shortfall-risk estimation, but it does not treat multivariate shortfall risk sets \(\mathcal R_\lambda(X)\subset\mathbb R^d\), capital allocation vectors \(m\in\mathbb R^d\), or online/streaming estimators for MSRM. Any extension to MSRM would require new arguments (e.g., uniform concentration for \(m\mapsto \mathbb E[\ell(-(X+m))]\) over \(m\in\mathbb R^d\), set estimation error metrics, and handling \(\ell\) on \(\mathbb R^d\)).

sources.title: An Alternating Direction Method of Multipliers for Utility-based Shortfall Risk Portfolio Optimization
sources.theorem_or_result:
The paper develops an ADMM algorithm for sample-average-approximation (SAA) utility-based shortfall risk (UBSR) portfolio optimization and proves convergence of the ADMM iterates for the finite-sample SAA problem. In particular, for the convex program
\[
\min_{w\in\mathbb R^n,t\in\mathbb R} (1-\alpha)t-\alpha\mu^\top w\ \text{s.t. } w\in W,\ \mu^\top w\ge R_0,\ \frac1m\sum_{i=1}^m l(-\xi_i^\top w-t)\le \lambda,
\]
Theorem 3.1 shows that the ADMM iterates \((w^k,t^k,z^k,s^k,\nu_1^k,\nu_2^k)\) satisfy objective residual and constraint violations decaying as \(O(1/(k+1))\):
- \((1-\alpha)(t^k-t^*)-\alpha\mu^\top(w^k-w^*)\to 0\)
- \(Rw^k+t^k\mathbf 1_m+z^k\to 0\), \(\mu^\top w^k-s^k-R_0\to 0\)
with explicit bounds \(\|Rw^k+t^k\mathbf 1_m+z^k\|^2+(\mu^\top w^k-s^k-R_0)^2\le C/(\sigma(k+1))\).
A key subroutine is fast projection onto \(Z=\{z\in\mathbb R^m: \frac1m\sum_{i=1}^m l(z_i)\le\lambda\}\), solved via semismooth Newton methods (Algorithms 2–4) with local Q-superlinear (and sometimes global) convergence guarantees for the projection KKT system / equivalent univariate root-finding formulation.
sources.connection:
The open problem asks for non-asymptotic finite-sample error bounds for *multivariate* shortfall risk estimators in a streaming/i.i.d. setting, i.e., bounds in sample size \(n\) comparing an online estimator \(\widehat{\mathcal R}_{\lambda,n}\) (or \(\hat m_n\)) to the population MSRM defined by \(\mathbb E[\ell(-(X+m))]\le\lambda\) for \(X\in\mathbb R^d\), \(d\ge2\).

This paper instead treats *univariate* UBSR \(\mathrm{SR}_\lambda(X)=\inf\{t: \mathbb E[l(-X-t)]\le\lambda\}\) and focuses on computational optimization for the *SAA* approximation with \(m\) scenarios. Its guarantees are iteration-complexity bounds in ADMM iteration count \(k\) for solving the finite-sample convex program, not statistical estimation error bounds in \(m\) (or streaming regret/MSE) relative to the population risk measure. It also does not address multivariate shortfall risk (vector allocations \(m\in\mathbb R^d\) with multivariate aggregation loss \(\ell:\mathbb R^d\to\mathbb R\)).

Nevertheless, the projection subproblem \(P_Z\) and semismooth-Newton/ADMM machinery are potentially useful tools if one designs online/SAA estimators for (multi)variate shortfall constraints, since MSRM estimation often reduces to solving convex feasibility/root-finding problems involving sample averages of convex losses.

sources.title: Some Studies on Stochastic Optimization based Quantitative Risk Management
sources.theorem_or_result: This article is primarily a survey/tutorial on stochastic-optimization-based quantitative risk management. It does not present new non-asymptotic finite-sample bounds for multivariate shortfall risk estimators. The main technical statements included are asymptotic central limit theorems for sample-average-approximation (SAA) estimators of (univariate) optimized certainty equivalent (OCE) risk and (univariate) utility-based shortfall risk (SR), e.g. for SR: if \,t_n\, solves the SAA constraint problem \(\min_{t\in T} t\) s.t. \(\frac1n\sum_{i=1}^n \ell(\xi_i-t)\le \lambda\), then \(\sqrt n (t_n-t^*)\Rightarrow N(0,\sigma^2)\) with \(\sigma^2=\mathrm{Var}(\ell(\xi-t^*))/\bigl(\mathbb E[\ell'(\xi-t^*)]\bigr)^2\) under differentiability/Lipschitz conditions (Theorem 1, attributed to Hu–Zhang 2018).
sources.connection: The open problem asks for non-asymptotic (finite-sample) error bounds for online/streaming estimators of multivariate shortfall risk measures (MSRM) for bounded \(X\in\mathbb R^d\), \(d\ge2\). This paper discusses (i) univariate OCE and univariate SR estimation/optimization mainly via SAA and stochastic approximation, providing asymptotic CLTs and consistency, and (ii) it explicitly points to Hegde et al. (2024) as providing finite-sample bounds for online SR (still univariate). It does not develop multivariate shortfall risk estimation, does not treat set-valued capital allocations \(\mathcal R_\lambda(X)\subset\mathbb R^d\), and does not provide finite-sample bounds for the multivariate case. Thus it offers background and pointers rather than progress on the stated multivariate finite-sample streaming-bounds problem.
